detail.title: arcforge finish Subcommand and allEpicsCompleted Helper

consumes.artifact: scripts/lib/coordinator.js (finish implementation will live alongside cleanup)

produces.artifact: New arcforge finish subcommand exposed via scripts/cli.js
New commit chore(specs): finish <spec-id> sprint when sprint-end runs
Pure helper allEpicsCompleted(dag) exported from scripts/lib/models.js

requirement.title: finish guards before any side effect
requirement.description: arcforge finish --spec-id <id> MUST verify a set of preconditions before performing any filesystem or git mutation. If any guard fails, the command MUST exit non-zero, print all failing guards to terminal (not just the first), and MUST NOT perform any cleanup, git rm, or commit. Guards: (1) specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml exists; (2) every epic in the dag has status=completed; (3) every epic branch is fully merged into the current branch; (4) the working tree is clean (no uncommitted modifications anywhere, including dag.yaml itself).

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml does not exist, When arcforge finish --spec-id <spec-id> runs, Then the command MUST exit non-zero with a message naming the missing file and MUST NOT delete any epics/ directory or create any commit. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R1.3 guard 1

Given a dag.yaml where at least one epic has status != "completed", When arcforge finish runs, Then the command MUST exit non-zero with a message listing every incomplete epic id and its actual status, and MUST NOT perform any mutation. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R1.3 guard 2

Given at least one epic branch is not fully merged into the current branch (per git branch --merged), When arcforge finish runs, Then the command MUST exit non-zero with a message listing each unmerged branch, and MUST NOT delete dag.yaml or epics/. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R1.3 guard 3

Given the working tree has any uncommitted modification (including to specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml), When arcforge finish runs, Then the command MUST exit non-zero with a message listing the dirty paths, and MUST NOT perform any mutation. With fr-cc-001 in effect there SHOULD be no drift at this point; the guard exists to surface anomalies rather than paper over them. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R1.3 guard 4 (tightened per Q1=c)

Given multiple guards fail simultaneously, When arcforge finish runs, Then the terminal output MUST list every failing guard (not stop at the first), so a user or skill retry loop can fix all blockers in one pass. design.md §Proposed Solution — Layer 1 (finish guards)

requirement.title: finish body deletes planner output and commits
requirement.description: After all guards in fr-cf-001 pass, arcforge finish MUST run (in order): (1) internal invocation of cleanup to remove any remaining completed-epic worktrees and fully-merged branches for the spec; (2) git rm specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml; (3) git rm -r specs/<spec-id>/epics/; (4) git commit with message chore(specs): finish <spec-id> sprint. The commit MUST contain exactly the dag.yaml and epics/ removals.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given all guards pass, When arcforge finish runs, Then after the command completes, specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml MUST NOT exist and specs/<spec-id>/epics/ MUST NOT exist on disk. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R1.4

Given arcforge finish produces a commit, When git log -1 --format=%s is read, Then the subject MUST equal chore(specs): finish <spec-id> sprint (with the actual spec-id substituted). design.md §Proposed Solution — Layer 1 (finish commit message)

Given arcforge finish produces a commit, When git log -1 --name-status is read, Then the changed files MUST be exactly specs/<spec-id>/dag.yaml (D) and all files previously under specs/<spec-id>/epics/ (D), with no other files included. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R1.4

Given at sprint end there are still completed-epic worktrees on disk or fully-merged epic branches locally, When arcforge finish runs and guards pass, Then the internal cleanup call MUST remove the remaining worktree directories and merged branches before the git rm step, so that sprint-end leaves no per-epic residue. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R1.4

requirement.title: finish --if-last flag for conditional execution
requirement.description: arcforge finish --spec-id <id> --if-last MUST first consult allEpicsCompleted(dag). If the predicate returns false, the command MUST exit zero with a short informational message (for example, "Not last epic — skipping finish") and MUST NOT perform any guard check or mutation. If the predicate returns true, the command MUST proceed as if --if-last were absent, running the guards in fr-cf-001 and the body in fr-cf-002. This flag lets skills call finish unconditionally at their terminus without needing to read dag state themselves.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a dag where not every epic has status=completed, When arcforge finish --spec-id <id> --if-last runs, Then the exit code MUST be zero, a single line message such as "Not last epic — skipping finish" MUST be printed, and NO guard failure messages MUST appear (the guards are not evaluated in this path). design.md §Identifiable Requirements R1.5

Given a dag where every epic has status=completed, When arcforge finish --spec-id <id> --if-last runs, Then the command MUST behave identically to arcforge finish --spec-id <id> without the flag: guards evaluated, and if all pass, body executed and commit produced. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R1.5

Given arcforge finish --spec-id <id> --if-last is invoked repeatedly while a sprint progresses, When not yet complete, the command MUST be safe to re-invoke as a no-op; when complete, the first invocation after completion MUST execute the full finish; subsequent invocations MUST fail cleanly at the dag.yaml-missing guard (fr-cf-001-ac1). design.md §Proposed Solution — Layer 2 (skill-friendly idempotence)

requirement.title: allEpicsCompleted helper in scripts/lib/models.js
requirement.description: A pure function allEpicsCompleted(dag) MUST be exported from scripts/lib/models.js. It takes a parsed dag object and returns a boolean: true when every epic in dag.epics has status equal to TaskStatus.COMPLETED, false otherwise (including when dag.epics is empty — an empty dag MUST return false to avoid accidental finish on a freshly-planned spec). The helper MUST be the single source of truth for the --if-last branch in fr-cf-003 and for any test that needs to assert sprint completeness.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Given a dag whose every epic has status="completed", When allEpicsCompleted(dag) is called, Then it MUST return true. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R1.6 (Q2a=models.js)

Given a dag with at least one epic whose status is not "completed" (pending / in_progress / blocked), When allEpicsCompleted(dag) is called, Then it MUST return false. design.md §Identifiable Requirements R1.6

Given a dag with an empty epics array, When allEpicsCompleted(dag) is called, Then it MUST return false, so that a freshly-created or zero-epic dag is never treated as a finished sprint. design.md §Proposed Solution — Layer 2 (guard edge case)

Given scripts/lib/models.js, When the module is imported, Then allEpicsCompleted MUST be a named export callable without any filesystem or git side effect (purity requirement), so it is safe to use from unit tests, CLI, and future integrations. design.md §Proposed Solution — Layer 2
